DASHBOARD: FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP

PermID4297411712Websitehttp://www.foxrothschild.com/IndustrySpecialized Maritime-shipping law Firm

ACTIVITIES:

Fox, Rothschild, O'Brien & Frankel, LLP provides legal services to businesses and individual clients throughout the greater Delaware Valley and central and southern New Jersey, as well as across the United States. The firms area of practice includes business, corporate and securities, labor and employment, real estate, creditors' rights, litigation, and tax and estates law. The firm was founded in 1907 and is based Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. It has offices in Doylestown, Exton, and Lansdale, Pennsylvania; Atlantic City, and Princeton, New Jersey; and Wilmington, Delaware.

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP [6 Press Releases]

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP CATHERINE MITCHELL AND THOMAS TERRELL SELECTED TO NORTH CAROLINA LAWYERS WEEKLY'S REAL ESTATE POWER LIST

Fox Rothschild congratulates Catherine B. Mitchell and Thomas E. Terrell Jr. who were named to North Carolina Lawyers Weekly's 2025 Real Estate Power List, which highlights North Carolina's leading real estate attorneys.

Mitchell, selected to the list for the second consecutive year, has handled more than $2 billion in transactions throughout her 14-year career. Mitchell is noted by North Carolina Lawyers Weekly as a "skilled advocate for national and regional clients in commercial real estate transactions throughout the development process." She advises clients on property tax exemptions for low-income housing and handles all aspects of commercial real estate transactions, including acquisitions, dispositions, leasing and financing. Her clients range from landlords and tenants to health care providers, developers and life insurance companies.

Mitchell is a Fellow of the American College of Mortgage Attorneys and a member of the University of North Carolina Asheville Board of Trustees. She also provides pro bono representation to businesses in Asheville that were affected by flooding from Hurricane Helene.

With four decades of practice, Terrell has handled zoning and land use matters in 88 of North Carolina's 100 counties, and in over 450 local governments across six states. He is hired in "hard-to-permit" industries, including mining, landfills and renewable energy.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP HEIDY VAQUERANO NAMED TO LA BUSINESS JOURNAL'S LEADERS OF INFLUENCE LIST

Fox Rothschild congratulates Heidy Vaquerano for being named to Los Angeles Business Journal's Leaders of Influence: Thriving in Their 40s list.

Vaquerano is among 40 individuals recognized for their skills and ability to "help set growth in motion for businesses in virtually any industry."

A partner in the firm's Entertainment & Sports Law Department, Vaquerano represents musicians, producers, actors, writers, production companies, record labels, tech startups, private-equity funds and other entertainment industry players.

She possesses a deep understanding of the evolving laws and technology impacting the music industry. Vaquerano works with artists and rights companies at the forefront of a major shift in the industry to help capitalize on market opportunities.

Notably, she has represented intellectual property-focused investment firm HarbourView Equity Partners since its founding in 2021. "In the past calendar year, she was instrumental in the client securing a combined $3 billion in debt financing through securitization for catalogues," the Los Angeles Business Journal reported. The debt financings were supported by more than 75 music catalogs from a variety of artists whose acquisitions were negotiated by Vaquerano and the Fox team.

Source: Company Website

+ GAMING PARTNER KIERON FRAZIER JOINS FOX ROTHSCHILD IN CHICAGO

Fox Rothschild welcomes Kieron A. Frazier to the Chicago office as a Partner in the Gaming Group.

"With Illinois' gaming industry continuing to boom, we are thrilled to welcome Kieron to our Chicago office," said Gordon E. Gouveia, Fox's Chicago Office Managing Partner. "Kieron's depth of experience in the gaming sector, combined with his unique background in sports law make him a valuable addition to our respected gaming industry team."

A corporate and transactional attorney, Frazier focuses his practice on the gaming industry.

He provides advice on regulatory compliance, licensure and internal investigations in addition to transactions and other general corporate matters. Frazier's clients include leaders in sports wagering, fantasy sports, iGaming, horse tracks, video gaming and lotteries. He also supports tribal licensees and social gaming operators and investors.

In addition, Frazier counsels clients on a variety of corporate and finance matters, including team-level transactional work, financing, media transactions, mergers and acquisitions.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD DONATES $1 MILLION TO SUPPORT YOUTH PROGRAMS THROUGH GENERATION FOX INITIATIVE

Fox Rothschild's Generation Fox community service initiative has donated $1 million, more than 5,000 items and 800+ service hours to more than 225 organizations that empower youth from coast to coast.

"Giving back has always been deeply personal to us and our firm culture - it's part of who we are," said Firmwide Managing Partner Todd A. Rodriguez. "With Generation Fox, we've put youth at the heart of our charitable efforts and community engagement. Our goal is to make a difference, and the results speak for themselves."

Launched in July 2024, Generation Fox invests in and supports youth in the communities where the firm has offices across the U.S. The initiative encompasses a multitude of ways that Fox gives back to its communities, with a focus on children up to age 18.

During the holidays, Generation Fox is stepping up its commitment to supporting local schools by providing essential supplies and financial assistance. Our offices have organized events or donation drives addressing the most urgent needs of schools in their communities.

"While every one of our offices has its own unique character, we are united by a shared purpose: to empower youth in our communities," said Holly Lentz Kleeman, Chief Business Development and Marketing Officer at Fox. "By collaborating with our local schools we are making a shared investment in the future."

Generation Fox was designed to be flexible so that lawyers, practice groups, offices and the firm's business professionals can each work and donate in ways that make sense in their cities and neighborhoods. The goals are equally expansive: to support groups that empower kids in their education and in their play, in cultural development, in nutrition, in health and well-being, and through protection of their legal rights.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP CAROLYN RICHMOND HONORED BY NEW YORK BUSINESS JOURNAL

Fox Rothschild congratulates Carolyn Richmond, who was recognized by the New York Business Journal as one of ten honorees who "exemplify influence in both their professional and their personal endeavors."

Part of the publication's Women of Influence program, Richmond's selection reflects her significant and lasting impact on the hospitality sector and her commitment to advancing workplace compliance and innovation.

Chair of the firm's Hospitality Practice, Richmond is a trusted employment attorney to New York's restaurant industry. She helps clients address evolving challenges, including wage and hour laws, workplace regulations, union organizing and crisis management.

Source: Company Website

+ FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP RICK COUGHLIN, JEFF MACHARG AND BRAD RISINGER SELECTED AS MANAGING PARTNERS TO WATCH

Fox Rothschild congratulates Greensboro, Charlotte and Raleigh Office Managing Partners, Richard A. Coughlin, Jeffrey P. MacHarg and Bradley M. Risinger, who were all recognized by North Carolina Lawyers Weekly as Managing Partners to Watch.

The annual feature spotlights managing partners who are "equal parts administrative genius and skilled legal practitioner."

Coughlin, selected for the second consecutive year, became Greensboro Managing Partner in 2021. Under his leadership the office has grown to become firm's largest in the Carolinas. Coughlin also serves as the firm's Deputy General Counsel where he plays an important part in helping lawyers navigate difficult situations. He told North Carolina Lawyers Weekly that, "if you put in enough effort and promote good communication, you usually can find a solution that benefits everyone." With an eye toward giving back to the community, Coughlin worked with the office to pledge $100,000 to support a Cone Health mobile health unit that will provide essential medical services to underserved communities in North Carolina.

Honoring his commitment to leadership and adaptability, this marks MacHarg's third year on the list. Since assuming the Charlotte Managing Partner role in 2021, he has doubled the number of attorneys in the office. MacHarg also led the team's relocation effort, moving the office into the heart of Charlotte's central business district. He maintains an active litigation practice with clients spanning a variety of industries. Colleagues continue to be inspired by the passion he brings to both roles, noting to North Carolina Lawyers Weekly that MacHarg remains "fully engaged and has made a significant impact on office life."

Risinger took the reins as Raleigh Managing Partner in 2022. He brings 35 years of legal experience to the role. A seasoned trial lawyer whose practice centers on commercial disputes, he is highlighted for representing law firms and media outlets denied access to public records by law enforcement - all cases that raise concerns about First Amendment rights and government transparency. A zealous advocate, Risinger says that "especially in today's climate, this is a fight that's always worth waging." North Carolina Lawyers Weekly also cited his involvement in local community organizations, including United Way of North Carolina and NC 211, the state's health and human services referral network.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JONES DAY [3 Press Releases]

+ FORMER SEC DEPUTY DIRECTOR NEKIA HACKWORTH JOINS JONES DAY AS PARTNER IN ATLANTA

Nekia Hackworth has joined Jones Day as a partner in its Investigations & White Collar Defense Practice. She is based in the Firm's Atlanta Office.

Ms. Hackworth has more than 20 years of experience in both government and private practice as a lawyer representing clients in white-collar criminal cases and civil litigation, conducting government and internal investigations, and counseling companies on corporate compliance matters. She served most recently as the Deputy Director of Enforcement in the Southeast Region for the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), where she supervised more than 250 professionals with responsibility for SEC matters across three offices (Washington, Atlanta, and Miami) and 11 jurisdictions. She oversaw hundreds of investigations in her tenure at the SEC, including suspected market manipulation, insider trading, and investment adviser fraud.

Source: Company Website

+ FORMER SEC DEPUTY DIRECTOR NEKIA HACKWORTH JOINS JONES DAY AS PARTNER IN ATLANTA

Nekia Hackworth has joined Jones Day as a partner in its Investigations & White Collar Defense Practice. She is based in the Firm's Atlanta Office.

Ms. Hackworth has more than 20 years of experience in both government and private practice as a lawyer representing clients in white-collar criminal cases and civil litigation, conducting government and internal investigations, and counseling companies on corporate compliance matters. She served most recently as the Deputy Director of Enforcement in the Southeast Region for the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), where she supervised more than 250 professionals with responsibility for SEC matters across three offices (Washington, Atlanta, and Miami) and 11 jurisdictions. She oversaw hundreds of investigations in her tenure at the SEC, including suspected market manipulation, insider trading, and investment adviser fraud.

Source: Company Website

+ DERIVATIVES LAWYER COLIN RICE JOINS JONES DAY IN SINGAPORE

Colin Rice has joined Jones Day as a partner in its Financial Markets Practice. He is based in the Firm's Singapore Office.

Mr. Rice is an experienced derivatives lawyer who has counseled clients on a broad range of financial markets transactions, with a particular focus on OTC derivatives, structured products, structured finance transactions, repos, and securities lending. He works with global investment banks, retail banks, funds, corporates, and service providers. He also advises on uncleared derivatives margin reform and collateral arrangements, default management, and the impact of global regulatory developments on derivatives businesses and documentation.

"Colin is an important addition to our global derivatives team. Asia is a fast-growing market with more and more netting friendly jurisdictions, and he is among the most experienced lawyers in the region on these matters," said Alban Caillemer du Ferrage, chair of Jones Day's Financial Markets department for EMEA and APAC. "Colin served as a director in the Singapore office of one of the world's largest investment banks. We will be able to work with our largest clients on their most complex derivatives transactions there. I am delighted to welcome him to our global group."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ STEPTOE LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ STEPTOE & JOHNSON LLP CONOR TUCKER APPOINTED CHAIR OF THE BEVERLY HILLS BAR ASSOCIATION'S APPELLATE LAW SECTION

(January 6, 2026, Los Angeles) - Steptoe LLP associate Conor Tucker has been appointed as Chair of the Beverly Hills Bar Association's Appellate Law Section for the 2025 - 2026 bar year.

Conor is an experienced litigator who has represented clients at every level of the federal court system - including serving as Counsel of Record before the United States Supreme Court. He has guided cases from the earliest stages of investigation through trial and appeal and has argued key motions and appeals in both state and federal courts. Conor's advocacy - in briefing and at argument- has led to published, precedent-setting decisions, including issues of first impression. In the California Court of Appeal alone, this includes litigating issues of first impression regarding the impact of COIVD-19 on force majeure clauses in live music performance contracts and state regulation of video streaming services. In the United States Supreme Court, he routinely serves as Counsel of Record for Members of Congress as amici.

He has handled a wide variety of matters, from contract disputes to intellectual property cases involving trademarks, trade secrets, copywrights, and patents, as well as constitutional, criminal procedure, and due process issues. His clients span a broad range of industries, including technology, energy, real estate, retail, apparel, mining, entertainment, banking, healthcare, social media, and computer sciences.

Source: Company Website

+ STEPTOE NAMED TO LEXOLOGY 100: DATA 2026

(December 22, 2025, Washington, DC) - Steptoe has been recognized in the 2026 edition of Lexology 100: Data, Lexology's annual guide profiling the world's leading data law firms. The Lexology 100: Data, formerly known as the GDR 100, is the only global ranking that evaluates firms' capabilities, track records, and market reputations across the full spectrum of data law, including privacy and data protection, AI regulation, online content moderation, cybersecurity, B2B data disputes, and the use of IP and confidentiality frameworks to safeguard proprietary information.

Lexology notes that the 150 firms recognized this year "fully merit the accolade," following a rigorous assessment process focused on firms' depth of practice, major matters, and sustained representation of companies with complex global data needs. The ranking reflects not only personal data work, but also the rapidly growing field of non‑personal data regulation, including AI‑related mandates, online harms laws, and cybersecurity frameworks such as DORA and NIS2.

Source: Company Website

+ STEPTOE & JOHNSON LLP THE HILL NAMES LESLIE BELCHER, ELIZABETH BURKS, AND ROWAN BOST TO TOP LOBBYISTS 2025

(December 12, 2025, Washington, DC) - Leslie Belcher, Elizabeth Burks, and Rowan Bost have been named to The Hill's Top Lobbyists 2025 list. Selected from more than 1,800 nominations the lobbyists on this list represent the industry's savviest, most influential and well-connected advocates across Capitol Hill. The honorees span veterans and up-and-comers, retired military officials and academics, former lawmakers and staffers, and are intended to reflect the wide ranges of experiences and backgrounds of the city's most influential K Street forces.

As Managing Director, Government Affairs & Public Policy at Steptoe, Leslie Belcher, an Oklahoma native, is a power-player and recognized trusted advisor to members of Congress. She offers clients more than 36 years of public and private sector experience, directing government relations campaigns for a variety of clients. Leslie has her finger on the pulse of the House Republican Conference and possesses an extensive knowledge of the legislative process. Her ability to navigate the complex and narrow majority of the GOP Conference, understand intricate policy details, and master the "art of the possible" is unmatched in Washington. Previously, Leslie served as Chief of Staff for Reps. Tom Cole (R-OK) (House Appropriations Chair) and Wes Watkins (R-OK), Legislative Director for Rep. Bill Brewster (D-OK), and Appropriations Associate for Rep. Wes Watkins (D-OK). This bipartisan experience provides Leslie with a unique and valuable perspective.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ATTORNEYS RECOGNIZED ON 2026 VIRGINIA SUPER LAWYERS 'RISING STARS LIST

McLEAN, Va. - April 28, 2026 - Three attorneys from global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP were selected for inclusion on the 2026 Virginia Super Lawyers "Rising Stars" list, recognizing outstanding young lawyers across a range of practice areas.

Super Lawyers evaluates attorneys statewide across more than 70 practice areas. The "Rising Stars" designation is awarded to no more than 2.5% of lawyers practicing in each state who demonstrate outstanding professional achievement and peer recognition early in their careers. Candidates are identified through a rigorous selection process that combines peer nominations with independent research and evaluation.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG TOKYO HOSTS ANREV BRIEFING ON REAL ASSETS, DATA CENTRES AND AI

TOKYO - April 30, 2026 - Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office recently hosted and sponsored a briefing for the Asian Association for Investors in Non‑Listed Real Estate Vehicles (ANREV), bringing together institutional investors and industry professionals to discuss emerging trends in real assets, data centres, and artificial intelligence.

The program featured a panel discussion titled "Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities," with Greenberg Traurig Shareholder Joel H. Rothstein, chair of the firm's Asia Real Estate Practice, among the speakers. Panelists discussed how advances in AI are reshaping capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies across global real asset markets, with a particular focus on the growing demand for data centre assets throughout the Asia‑Pacific region.

+ MORGAN LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ MORGAN LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP EU PAY TRANSPARENCY DIRECTIVE: THE COUNTDOWN IS ON, HR EXECUTIVE

Partner Louise Skinner and associate Margaret McDowell were quoted by HR Executive in an article examining the approaching compliance deadline for the European Union's Pay Transparency Directive and its implications for multinational employers.

Louise emphasized the need for employers to proactively assess hiring practices and prepare for increased transparency, noting that "multinationals with European operations should assess whether current recruitment processes are compliant with the new requirements that apply during the hiring process."

Margaret highlighted compliance challenges arising from varying US requirements, explaining that "some of the most consequential differences that we have seen be overlooked include which roles are covered. when the disclosure obligation is triggered and what information must be disclosed," and cautioning that "the cost of getting this wrong can be significant."

Source: Company Website

+ INTERNATIONAL EMPLOYMENT LAWYER NAMES MORGAN LEWIS'S LAKEISHA ANDRESS AMONG 2026 TOMORROW'S LEADERS

DALLAS, April 28, 2026: Morgan Lewis of counsel Lakeisha Andress has been named to the International Employment Lawyer list of 2026 Tomorrow's Leaders. The annual program highlights legal professionals from around the world who have been involved in high-profile or innovative disputes, projects, or deals during their first decade of practice.

Lakeisha represents employers in a wide range of complex employment matters, including sensitive and high-profile internal investigations, strategic counseling, and litigation. She conducts investigations into allegations of workplace misconduct at all company levels, such as discrimination, sexual harassment, hostile work environment, and whistleblower claims. She also strategically advises employers on all aspects of the workplace.

Source: Company Website

+ MORGAN LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP FTC STEPS UP ENFORCEMENT OF "MADE IN USA" LABELING RULE, FTCWATCH

FTCWatch quoted associate Caiti Zeytoonian in an article examining the renewed enforcement efforts by the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) of its "Made in USA" Labeling Rule.

The article discusses a series of recent FTC complaints targeting companies accused of falsely marketing imported goods as US-made, signaling a more coordinated enforcement approach. Caiti noted that the agency's focus reflects both consumer protection priorities and the broader significance of origin claims in the marketplace.

"The agency wants to protect consumers and prioritize preserving the importance and the value of 'Made in USA' claims," Caiti said. "An American flag, an American flagpole, is the quintessential American product."

urn:contentItem:6JHD-YCV3-SHN4-P37X-00000-00DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - SECTOR NEWS AND PEER UPDATES2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:39ZLexisNexisSTOCKDAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - SECTOR NEWS AND PEER UPDATES

DASHBOARD: DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP

PermID4296380340Websitehttp://www.davispolk.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress450 Lexington Avenue New York, NY 10017 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP, founded in 1849, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 1,000 lawyers across 10 offices, it serves clients in finance, technology, and healthcare, specializing in M&A, capital markets, and regulatory matters. In 2020, it advised on Morgan Stanleys $13 billion E*Trade acquisition and Gilead Sciences $21 billion Immunomedics deal. In 2024, it expanded its Menlo Park office with key hires like Paul Scrivano from Ropes & Gray. Generating over $1.5 billion annually, its culture emphasizes collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work. Competing with Cravath, Davis Polk stands out for its dealmaking prowess, ranking second in 2020 M&A league tables. Recent initiatives include AI-driven legal tech and ESG advisory services.

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIGENERGY'S HK IPO GAINS MOMENTUM WITH DAVIS POLK'S LEGAL GUIDANCE

Sigenergy Technology, a Chinese energy storage systems maker, has completed its Hong Kong IPO, raising HK$4.4 billion (about $562 million). The deal was guided by Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, which provided crucial legal counsel throughout the process. The listing was recorded on the 17th of April 2026. Founded in May 2022 and based in Shanghai, Sigenergy specializes in distributed energy storage systems and develops stackable, solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions. The company holds a 28.6 percent share of the global market for stackable, all-in-one DESS products, underscoring its leadership in a fast-growing sector. The offering was underwritten by a consortium including CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with CICC acting as joint sponsor. Legal support for the underwriters came from Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ Christine Xu, Lipton Li, and Queenie Tong advising. In related news, Mindspace Business Parks REIT’s Chennai IT Park acquisition for 30 billion rupees was advised by Khaitan & Co, while SBI Funds Management is preparing for a 130-billion-rupee IPO with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising. The Sigenergy IPO highlights rising investor interest in renewables and energy storage.

+ DAVIS POLK EXPANDS WEST COAST FOOTPRINT WITH LOS ANGELES OFFICE OPENING

Davis Polk & Wardwell said on 14th April 2026 that it will establish a Los Angeles office, appointing Jason Russell to lead the expansion. The move underscores the firm's bid to grow its West Coast footprint.Russell, a veteran litigator who previously ran Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom's Los Angeles office and served on its policy committee, brings a portfolio of high-profile clients including the NBA, Christie’s and MGA Entertainment that the firm hopes to leverage in California.Davis Polk plans to hire additional lawyers for the new LA office and to accelerate its local capabilities, signaling an intent to compete with other leading California firms and win significant matters as the market heats up.

+ LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL, BACKED BY DAVIS POLK, NETS $2.5 BILLION FOR SIXTH FUND

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has raised $2.5 billion for its sixth fund, a milestone that underscores investor confidence in its strategy. The announcement came on April 14, 2026.The fund will target investments in financial services and technology, sectors that the firm sees as resilient and growth-focused. Davis Polk's legal services experience for financial institutions complements Lightyear's effort to identify opportunities and execute deals, reinforcing the collaboration between the two entities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LONDON OFFICE REPORTS RECORD REVENUE OF $268 MILLION, UP 40%

Debevoise & Plimpton has reported a remarkable revenue surge of 40% in its London office, reaching a record $268 million. The results, announced on 7th April 2026, underscore the firm's strength in a competitive market.The growth is driven by higher demand for corporate law and dispute resolution services, with the firm's strategy of pursuing high-value client engagements and its ability to adapt to changing market conditions cited as key factors.Analysts expect the momentum to continue as Debevoise plans to invest in talent and broaden its service offerings, a strategy that could attract more clients and sustain revenue gains in the years ahead.

+ DEBEVOISE SECURES SECOND CIRCUIT AFFIRMATION FOR YPF IN $16 BILLION EXPROPRIATION CASE

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP secured an affirmance from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit on behalf of YPF S.A., the largest energy producer in Argentina. In a decision made on 30th March 2026, the court dismissed claims for more than $16 billion in damages and interest, ending a dispute that has lingered in the Southern District ofNew York since 2015. The ruling confirms the district court's assessment that YPF bore no liability for the asserted obligations.

+ DEBEVOISE ELEVATES SULLIVAN KC AND POPOVA TO EUROPEAN DISPUTES CO-CHAIRS

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has appointed Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately, expanding the firm's footprint in Paris, Frankfurt and Luxembourg. The appointment was announced on the 17th of March, 2026, underscoring Debevoise's commitment to strengthening international dispute resolution across mainland Europe.Catherine Amirfar, Co-Chair of Debevoise's global International Dispute Resolution Group, noted that Sullivan and Popova will build on the firm's long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe and will continue delivering the highest level of advocacy and strategic counsel for clients. Sullivan KC is widely recognized for his work in international arbitration and public international law, focusing on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations; Popova brings broad international arbitration experience, dual civil and common-law qualifications, fluency in several languages, and previously served on the ICCInternational Court of Arbitration. Debevoise's International Dispute Resolution Group has more than four decades of experience and has handled matters across over 125 countries, with stakes exceeding $110 billion.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ RODGE COHEN REFLECTS ON FIVE DECADES SHAPING BANKING AT SULLIVAN & CROMWELL

H. Rodgin "Rodge" Cohen, the senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has been a transformative force in banking for more than five decades. On 19th February 2026, at an NYU Law Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF) event, Cohen spoke with candor about the arc of the modern financial system, the discussion joined by Jay Clayton, former chair of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, and Edward Rock, ICGF co-director.Cohen traced regulatory evolution from the postwar era to the present, emphasizing how landmark measures reshaped access to banking. He and Clayton highlighted the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act as turning points that broadened inclusion and redefined compliance burdens. Clayton noted the dramatic shifts since the 1970s, while Cohen underscored how these changes altered banks’ operations and risk profiles.Beyond regulation, Cohen’s influence extended to crisis work. He recalled the 2008 financial crisis, representing several major banks as policymakers moved to stabilize the system. Citing the hours before the government’s intervention following Lehman Brothers’ collapse, Cohen said, "We were on the edge of a cliff," a moment that underscored the urgency of decisive action and cemented his reputation as a leading financial-services lawyer. He also highlighted interstate banking moves in the 1980s that helped banks operate across state lines, reshaping the landscape. Clayton praised Cohen’s ability to interpret statutes creatively, a skill essential for navigating modern regulation.Looking ahead, Cohen warned that cryptocurrency regulation must stay in step with rapid market change. He cautioned that regulators risk creating weak rules if they lag behind innovation, invoking Gresham’s Law to argue that "Bad regulation drives out good regulation." The conversation underscored the ongoing evolution of the financial sector and reaffirmed the central role of firms like Sullivan & Cromwell in shaping its future.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL ELEVATES LONDON FINANCE PRACTICE WITH TWO NEW PARTNERS

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP announced the appointment of Christopher McLaughlin and Alastair McVeigh as partners in its London office, expanding the firm’s private capital capabilities across the UK and Europe. The hires, disclosed in a Jan. 21, 2026 release, align with the firm’s ongoing UK and European expansion plans. McLaughlin will lead private equity sponsor and borrower acquisitions as well as leveraged finance in London, while McVeigh will head the European private credit offering with a focus on direct and specialty lending.Robert Giuffra and Scott Miller, Co-Chairs of Sullivan & Cromwell, welcomed the new partners and highlighted how their expertise will strengthen the firm’s ability to serve private equity sponsors, lenders, and private credit providers throughout the region.McLaughlin is recognized as a leading voice in acquisition and leveraged finance law in Europe, with extensive experience advising private equity sponsors and private credit providers. He has been ranked as a Leading Individual for Leveraged Finance by Chambers UK and as a Leading Partner for Acquisition Finance by Legal 500. McVeigh is likewise respected for his work in leveraged finance, particularly in private credit and specialty lending, making him a strong addition to the firm’s growing practice.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB [3 News That Matters]

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB EXPANDS DIGITAL ASSETS TEAM WITH IGOR KLEYMAN RETURN

Cleary Gottlieb announced the reappointment of Igor Kleyman as a partner in its financial institutions practice, expanding the firm's focus on digital assets, artificial intelligence, and fintech. The firm disclosed the move on 23rd April 2026.Kleyman returns after nearly a decade advising clients on digital assets and AI. Most recently, he led Amazon's Responsible AI Governance, and previously held key roles at Nasdaq and Gemini Trust Company, where he navigated regulatory challenges and contributed to the development of pioneering financial products.Managing Partner Jeff Karpf expressed enthusiasm about Igor's return, stating, "Igor's fluency across the transformational fintech and AI sectors, combined with his sophisticated understanding of financial regulatory structures and institutions, enhances our powerhouse of innovation in financial services." This sentiment underscores the firm's strategy to remain at the forefront of legal services in these dynamic fields. Igor Kleyman added, "I look forward to collaborating across Cleary's global network to further strengthen our relationships with fintech companies and businesses exploring digital assets, tokenization, and AI offerings."

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB REINSTATES DIGITAL ASSETS AND AI SPECIALIST FROM AMAZON

Cleary Gottlieb announced the return of a digital assets and AI specialist who previously held a senior role at Amazon. The hire signals the firm's ongoing effort to deepen its capabilities in the fast-changing digital economy, where AI and digital asset management are increasingly intertwined. The professional's prior work at Amazon included leadership on AI initiatives and digital-asset programs, according to people familiar with the matter.On 20th April 2026, the firm confirmed that the specialist has rejoined after a successful tenure at Amazon. The move comes as legal frameworks governing digital currencies and AI applications become more complex, creating a need for experienced guidance. Cleary Gottlieb says the added expertise will strengthen its advisory work for clients in fintech, regulatory compliance, and related fields.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB UNVEILS CX+, AN AI PLATFORM TO OPTIMIZE LEGAL WORKFLOWS

Cleary Gottlieb has announced the launch of CX+, a proprietary AI-powered platform designed to enhance legal workflows through advanced contract intelligence and due diligence capabilities. The announcement was made on the 15th of April 2026, and CX+ is comprised of two main products: CX+Insights and CX+Transact, which cater specifically to the operational and transactional needs of in-house legal teams and deal professionals.Developed in collaboration with senior in-house clients and legal operations experts, CX+ was crafted to address the challenges faced by legal teams. Carla Swansburg, CEO of ClearyX, said, "We spent years speaking with clients about the problems that they wish technology would solve for them, and that generic tools simply aren't solving." The platform is designed to be adaptable and model-agnostic, allowing clients to configure it to their workflows, with emphasis on security and API integrations that enable immediate value from the outset.CX+ is split into two offerings: CX+Insights, which pulls in contracts to deliver AI-driven questions and dashboards, and CX+Transact, which supports contract review and M&A due diligence with tagging, filters and in-place editing. ClearyX also notes that customers can license CX+ as a standalone tool or alongside ClearyX services, and that the platform has demonstrated effective time and cost savings on diligence tasks across more than 150 deals, highlighting the platform's potential to streamline client service.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LATHAM & WATKINS AND LOEB & LOEB LEAD $250 MILLION SPAC DEAL FOR AI BATTERY COMPANY

Two leading law firms, Latham & Watkins and Loeb & Loeb, are guiding the $250 million merger of AI Battery Company with a SPAC. The deal is designed to bolster the battery company's finances and speed its growth in the market for AI-driven energy storage solutions. The merger is expected to close shortly after regulatory approvals.The deal, announced on 21st April 2026, positions AI Battery Company to push ahead with AI-enabled battery technology, aiming to improve efficiency and sustainability while expanding manufacturing capabilities. The transaction provides capital for intensified R&D and scaling production.Latham & Watkins brings expertise in SPAC transactions and capital markets, while Loeb & Loeb contributes regulatory compliance and corporate governance guidance. Investors are watching the process as demand for AI-integrated solutions remains strong, and the SPAC route offers a clearer path to public ownership. The parties are optimistic about a smooth close and continued growth in the AI battery space.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS REPORTS RECORD GLOBAL REVENUE OF $8.3 BILLION FOR FY2025

Latham & Watkins LLP reported record results for fiscal year 2025, with global gross revenue of $8.3 billion, an 18.6% rise from about $7 billion in 2024. The results were announced on April 16, 2026, alongside the firm’s submissions for the 2026 Am Law 100 rankings released on the same day.Profit per equity partner rose to about $8.7 million, up 21.3% from roughly $7.1 million in 2024, underscoring the firm’s robust profitability.A key highlight is Latham’s London office, which crossed the $1 billion revenue threshold for the first time, up from about $850 million in 2024, signifying growth of more than 17%. The London office now employs around 1,000 staff and remains the firm’s largest non-U.S. location as it executes its Europe-focused growth strategy.The firm benefited from strong demand in core practices, including mergers and acquisitions, private equity, finance, and capital markets. Latham led nearly 800 M&A deals in 2025 with combined values exceeding $720 billion, and revenue per lawyer is estimated at about $2.23 million for 2025, based on a headcount of roughly 3,700 lawyers. These results come amid Am Law 100 trends showing broader revenue growth and improving profitability across leading firms.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS REPORTS RECORD $8.3 BILLION REVENUE, ELEVATING ITS GLOBAL STANDING

Latham & Watkins on 15th April 2026 reported global revenue of $8.3 billion, marking a new firm record and reinforcing its status as one of the world’s most influential law firms.The surge reflects broad demand across core practices, with clients seeking complex litigation, regulatory counsel, and large-scale transactions. Latham’s ability to scale across borders while sustaining profitability signals a market environment that rewards high-value work and sophisticated advisory capabilities.Revenue gains came notably from transactional disciplines such as capital markets and private equity, along with stronger activity in restructuring and finance. The firm’s diversified, leverage-driven model and global footprint have supported steady growth, and leadership suggests continued hiring and competitive compensation to attract top talent as competition for top lawyers intensifies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P. [3 News That Matters]

+ SKADDEN PROMOTES NINE LONDON PARTNERS AS NON-EQUITY TIER DEBUTS GLOBALLY

Skadden has promoted nine London partners as part of a 55-strong global round, the firm's first since it introduced a non-equity partner tier. The step reflects a broader move among US firms to refine compensation structures and retain top talent.This year's cohort more than doubles last year's 22, with London's intake rising markedly from the City's historically modest rounds.The nine new London partners are Jonathan Benson in White Collar Defense and Investigations, Zoe Q. Cooper Sutton in Finance, Benjamin T. Davies in Corporate, Kara L. Heggs in Tax, Jason Hewitt and Nicola Kerr-Shaw in National Security, Feargal Ryan in Financial Institutions, Kenneth J. Ryan in Capital Markets, and Cristina Tomassini in Corporate.

+ SKADDEN REPRESENTS OTSUKA IN USD 1.2 BILLION ACQUISITION OF TRANSCEND THERAPEUTICS

Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom is advising Tokyo-based Otsuka Pharmaceutical on its acquisition of clinical-stage biotechnology company Transcend Therapeutics in a deal potentially valued at USD1.2 billion. As of 1st April 2026, the transaction is set to bolster Otsuka's portfolio in the competitive biotech landscape.Under the terms of the agreement, Otsuka will pay USD700 million to Transcend shareholders upon completion, anticipated in the second quarter of 2026. In addition, Otsuka could pay up to USD525 million in contingent consideration based on future sales milestones tied to assets currently in development.Transcend is being advised by New York-based Kirkland & Ellis, led by partners Graham Robinson and Laura Knoll. They are supported by Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman partner Ron Fleming Jr. The financial advisory for Transcend has been handled by Centerview Partners, with partners Andrew S Rymer and Jason Zuckerbrod, alongside principal Tommaso Verderame. The Skadden engagement underscores the growing pace of biotech mergers and acquisitions as companies seek to bolster capabilities amid regulatory complexities and evolving market dynamics.

+ ALICORP EXPANDS LATIN AMERICAN FOOTPRINT AS SIMPSON THACHER AND SKADDEN ADVISE ON FLORA FOOD GROUP DEAL

Simpson Thacher and Skadden have advised Alicorp on the acquisition of Flora Food Group's brand portfolio, a move that will widen Alicorp's reach across 17 Latin American markets, excluding Mexico and Brazil, and secure a manufacturing facility in Cali, Colombia. The deal was announced on 18th March 2026.The transaction is part of Alicorp's broader aim to diversify its offerings and strengthen its competitive position by adding established brands and operational capabilities. The involvement of Simpson Thacher and Skadden underscores the cross-border nature of the deal and the importance of experienced M&A counsel for Alicorp's regional growth.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS [3 News That Matters]

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS HIRES PRIVATE CAPITAL SPECIALIST WILL AITKEN-DAVIES TO STRENGTHEN LONDON PRACTICE

Kirkland & Ellis has announced the addition of Will Aitken-Davies as a corporate partner in its London office, expanding its private capital practice. The hire is effective immediately and aims to strengthen capabilities in private equity and infrastructure investor transactions across European and cross-border markets. The announcement was made on 29th April 2026 at 16:34:10.Aitken-Davies joins from Paul Weiss, bringing experience in complex acquisitions, carve-outs, co-investments, public M&A, consortium deals, and hybrid equity investments. His sector coverage includes energy and infrastructure, industrials, technology, and financial services; he has been ranked in Chambers UK for Private Equity Buyouts, as well as in Legal 500 UK and IFLR1000.Jon A. Ballis, Chairman of the Executive Committee at Kirkland & Ellis, described Will as a trusted adviser to leading financial investors on sophisticated, cross-border European transactions, and said his arrival strengthens the firm’s market-leading team’s ability to support sponsors executing at scale across London and Europe. Executive Committee Member Matthew Elliott added that Aitken-Davies is a market-leading dealmaker whose European M&A experience will enhance the firm’s integrated advisory capabilities.

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS EXPANDS HONG KONG LITIGATION PRACTICE WITH HIRE OF NEW PARTNER

Kirkland & Ellis has announced the appointment of Wilfred Ho as a partner in its Hong Kong litigation practice, a move designed to strengthen the firm’s capabilities across the Asia-Pacific region. The announcement was made on 29th April 2026, underscoring the firm's commitment to delivering enhanced cross-border dispute resolution services.Ho joins from White & Case in Hong Kong, where he was a partner focusing on international arbitration and commercial litigation. With more than a decade of experience advising Asian corporates and private equity funds on cross-border disputes across Greater China, he previously honed his practice at Skadden. Ho's work covers shareholder disputes, insolvency and restructuring, and intellectual property, serving clients in gaming, media, entertainment, hospitality, technology and energy. In arbitration, he has represented clients under HKIAC, CIETAC, ICC, NAI and SIAC rules. Neil McDonald, a Kirkland & Ellis partner, described Ho as a transformative addition to the Asia litigation team.

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS EXPANDS PHILADELPHIA FOOTPRINT WITH ADAM PRINCE APPOINTMENT

Kirkland & Ellis announced the hire of Adam Prince as a partner in its Corporate Practice Group in Philadelphia, effective 27th April 2026. Prince will bolster the firm's transactional capabilities, with a focus on private equity, mergers and acquisitions, investments, recapitalizations and other complex transactions, particularly in the healthcare sector.Prince previously was a Morgan Lewis partner, advising private equity sponsors, portfolio companies and growth-stage businesses on transactions ranging from early-stage investments to multibillion-dollar deals and serving as outside general counsel to growth-stage companies.The Philadelphia office, launched in 2025, has grown to about 20 lawyers across litigation and transactional practices. Jon A. Ballis, Chairman of the Executive Committee, welcomed Prince, saying, "Adam is a terrific lawyer with a stellar reputation both in private equity and in the Philadelphia community, and he is the perfect fit to help build our transactional practice as we grow in the city." Leo Greenberg, a Corporate Partner, noted the benefits of adding Prince alongside Stephanie Haas as the firm expands its Philadelphia presence. Prince himself said, "Kirkland is no doubt the premier law firm for sophisticated private equity work. Combining this platform with the chance to build a top team here in Philadelphia with Steph and all of the welcoming, energetic and collaborative partners I’ve met so far, I couldn’t be more excited to take on this opportunity."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR TO TAKE TAIYO HOLDINGS PRIVATE IN $3.3 BILLION TENDER OFFER BACKED BY MAJOR STAKEHOLDERS

KKR has announced plans to acquire all outstanding shares of Taiyo Holdings for 528.56 billion yen, about $3.3 billion, through a tender offer. The bid is supported by key shareholders and is being advised by Simpson Thacher & Bartlett. The announcement was made on 10th April 2026.The offer price of 4,750 yen per share represents a 4.7% discount to Taiyo's last closing price. Backers including DIC Corp, Kowa Co and Oasis Management together hold 42.2% of Taiyo's outstanding shares and have signaled strong support for KKR's privatization plan.Simpson Thacher & Bartlett is advising KKR, with a team led by partners Noritaka Kumamoto and Jonathan Stradling, along with Daisuke Ueta and Kaoruko Sakuramoto on mergers and acquisitions. Antitrust matters are being handled by partner Etienne Renaudeau and associates Dani Rabinowitz, Luke Cowdell and Giovanna De Cristofaro, while credit-related issues are led by partner Makiko Harunari, counsel Atsushi Usui and associate Mark Watson.

+ SIMPSON THACHER GUIDES NSSK TO RECORD NSSK IV FUND FORMATION

Simpson Thacher & Bartlett advised the Japanese private equity firm Nippon Sangyo Suishin Kiko (NSSK) on forming the NSSK IV Series Funds, securing total commitments of JPY250 billion (USD1.5 billion). The milestone was reached on 10th April 2026, with the offering closing on 31st March 2026.The global team provided comprehensive counsel, focusing on regulatory and compliance matters essential to the fund's formation. Leading partners included David Azcue, co-head of the private funds practice in Japan; Adam Furber, co-head of the Asia offices and head of the Asia investment funds practice; and Makiko Harunari, head of the Asia banking and credit practice.The NSSK IV Series Funds were more than twice oversubscribed, attracting commitments from over 100 institutional investors worldwide. This level of demand underscores strong investor confidence in NSSK's strategy and signals growing interest in private equity in the Asia-Pacific region.

+ SIMPSON THACHER EXPANDS CAPITAL STRUCTURE SOLUTIONS WITH NEW PARTNER ADDITIONS

Simpson Thacher & Bartlett announced the addition of partners Christine Bae and Jacob Ruby to its Capital Structure Solutions Practice, reinforcing the firm's commitment to complex financing and restructuring matters as the practice expands after its early-2026 launch. The announcement, dated 18th March 2026, underscores the firm's plan to broaden advisory capabilities in liability management, private credit, and special situations under the leadership of David Nemecek.Bae and Ruby bring extensive experience advising sponsors, companies, lenders, and investors on intricate capital-structure challenges. The Capital Structure Solutions Practice now integrates the firm’s teams in liability management, special situations, and restructuring with its broader financing capabilities to provide coordinated guidance across financing strategy, credit solutions, distressed M&A, and Chapter 11 restructurings. David Nemecek stated, "Christine and Jacob bring deep technical capability, sound judgment, and the ability to craft bespoke solutions for clients navigating their most complex capital structure challenges." Both hires also emphasized Simpson Thacher's collaborative culture and integrated platform as key factors supporting their practice.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WACHTELL, LIPTON, ROSEN & KATZ LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SYSCO HIRES JONES DAY AND WACHTELL FOR $29 BILLION JETRO ACQUISITION

Sysco Corporation has engaged Jones Day for antitrust counsel as it moves to acquire rival Jetro Restaurant Depot for a $29 billion deal. The law firm Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz LLP will advise on the merger, and the appointment was confirmed on 31st March 2026.The transaction is intended to bolster Sysco’s leadership in the competitive food distribution market, which has faced rising costs and supply chain pressures. With Jetro’s established network in the restaurant-supply sector, Sysco aims to enhance operations and broaden its customer base, while industry observers expect a thorough regulatory review given the deal’s size.Jones Day’s antitrust practice is well-regarded for guiding major deals, and Wachtell’s experience in complex mergers will be central to navigating the legal landscape of this transaction. The collaboration underscores the importance of seasoned counsel in large-scale consolidations and is likely to influence how regulators approach similar deals in the sector.

+ WACHTELL LIPTON GUIDES PUBLIC STORAGE IN $10.5 BILLION ALL-STOCK ACQUISITION OF NATIONAL STORAGE AFFILIATES

Public Storage announced on 24th March 2026 that it will acquire National Storage Affiliates in a $10.5 billion all-stock transaction. Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz LLP is advising Public Storage, the largest self-storage company in the United States by market capitalization, and Clifford Chance is representing National Storage Affiliates in this all-stock deal.Under the agreement, National Storage shareholders will receive 0.14 of a Public Storage common share for each National Storage share, valuing the consideration at $41.68 per share. The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, after the formation of a joint venture that will encompass 313 properties with an estimated value of about $3.3 billion, expanding Public Storage's footprint in key markets. Real estate financing counsel to Public Storage is provided by DLA Piper, led by Amy Carbins and supported by Viktor Sapezhnikov.

+ X CORP DROPS $90 MILLION SUIT AGAINST WACHTELL LIPTON, ENDS LEGAL BATTLE

In a swift legal pivot, Elon Musk’s X Corp has voluntarily dismissed its high-stakes $90 million lawsuit against powerhouse law firm Wachtell Lipton Rosen & Katz, closing a bitter chapter from the chaotic 2022 Twitter takeover. The lawsuit, filed in San FranciscoSuperior Court back on 1st October 2023, accused Wachtell of pocketing an 'improper bonus payment' that violated fiduciary duties, claiming Twitter executives inflated fees with 'success' bonuses just before the $44 billion deal closed.Wachtell Lipton, which represented the old Twitter board in the fierce battle to compel Musk to honor his buyout pledge, responded to the accusations with vigor. The firm emphasized its 'complete success' after months of litigation, which ultimately secured billions for shareholders. They asserted that the fees in question were approved by the board itself, effectively countering the claims made by X Corp.The dismissal of the lawsuit, which was revealed in a court filing reported by Reuters, bars refiling and indicates a clean break between the two parties. A spokesperson for Wachtell Lipton expressed satisfaction with the outcome, stating, 'We are pleased that X has dismissed its meritless lawsuit with prejudice. There was no settlement.' This marks a significant turn in the ongoing legal saga surrounding the Twitter acquisition.This legal retreat comes on the heels of another reconciliation for X Corp, which recently acknowledged a $128 million executive payout suit related to the tumultuous period during the Twitter acquisition. The dismissal signals a potential shift in strategy for X Corp, as it seeks to distance itself from the financial and legal entanglements of its predecessor.As the legal landscape continues to evolve for Elon Musk's X Corp, the resolution with Wachtell Lipton may pave the way for a more focused approach to its business operations. Investors and market observers will be keenly watching how these developments influence the company's trajectory moving forward.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CRAVATH, SWAINE & MOORE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CRAVATH PARTNER LAUNCHES BOUTIQUE DEDICATED TO CORPORATE INVESTIGATIONS

A Cravath Swaine & Moore partner has launched a boutique law firm dedicated to corporate investigations, signaling a shift toward specialized counsel as companies navigate increasingly complex regulatory demands. The new practice will offer internal investigations, compliance assessments, and risk management guidance, leveraging the founder’s Cravath experience to tailor services to clients under scrutiny.On 9th March 2026, the launch underscored a wider trend of seasoned practitioners leaving traditional firm structures to build agile, focused practices. The boutique aims to serve clients facing intricate corporate reviews with a high-touch, confidentiality-forward approach that larger firms often struggle to match.Observers anticipate a diverse roster, including Fortune 500 companies, private equity firms, and startups. The founder’s network and Cravath pedigree are expected to lend immediate credibility, while the emphasis on discretion and bespoke handling aims to differentiate the firm in a crowded market.

+ BIOCON SUCCEEDS $1.2 BILLION BIOSIMILARS DEAL, ENGAGING MULTIPLE LAW FIRMS

Biocon, the Indian biopharmaceutical company, has closed a $1.2 billion deal to fully consolidate its biosimilars operations by acquiring the remaining stakes from minority partners. The transaction, disclosed on December 11, 2025, is designed to streamline Biocon's footprint in a fast expanding biosimilars market and strengthen its growth trajectory.Viatris agreed to divest its remaining stake for about $815 million as part of the package, while other sellers include True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II. Serum Institute Life Sciences was among the sellers, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas advising it. Khaitan & Co and Cravath, Swaine & Moore advised Viatris on financing matters, and Veritas Legal provided counsel to True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II.Biocon tapped Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas to lead its advisory team, with Raghubir Menon and Natashaa Shroff at the helm. The firm also included Tarumoy Chaudhuri and Kriti Saraswat. The deal aligns with Biocon's ongoing integration with Viatris and its debt refinancing plans, underscoring the company's push to consolidate its biosimilars business amid a competitive market.

+ CRAVATH SWAINE & MOORE ANNOUNCES YEAR-END BONUSES, ASSOCIATES ELIGIBLE FOR UP TO $140,000

Cravath Swaine & Moore, one of the largest U.S. law firms, has officially launched the year-end bonus season by announcing that its associates will receive payouts of up to $140,000. On 20th November 2025, the New York-based firm communicated to its associates via an internal memo that annual bonuses will range from $15,000 for first-year associates to $115,000 for the most senior associates, according to information reported by Above the Law.In addition to the standard bonuses, Cravath plans to offer special bonuses ranging from $6,000 to $25,000, mirroring the payouts recently made by rival firm Milbank. This strategic move reinforces Cravath’s position as a traditional first mover in the competitive landscape of associate compensation within the legal sector. While the firm declined to provide further comments, the bonus structure aligns with expectations set by previous years.Following Cravath's announcement, other major firms are already responding. Paul Hastings, for instance, quickly disclosed its own year-end and special bonuses, which reflect a similar scale to those set by Cravath. A spokesperson from Paul Hastings also chose not to comment, indicating that the firm is likely following the trend established by Cravath.The bonuses announced by Cravath Swaine & Moore are consistent with those distributed by large U.S. law firms last year, as many associates now earn annual salaries ranging from $225,000 to $435,000 depending on their class year. The competitive nature of the legal profession often leads firms to match salary and bonus figures to retain top talent, and Cravath’s decision is expected to influence other firms in the sector.Looking ahead, U.S. law firms are poised for a profitable 2025, with large and midsized firms experiencing a significant increase in client demand, particularly in transactional practices. This positive outlook was highlighted in a recent analysis of firm financial data, suggesting that the legal industry is gearing up for another successful year, further emphasizing the importance of competitive compensation packages like those offered by Cravath.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ MILBANK UNVEILS 12-YEAR PLAN TO SHIELD ASH TREES FROM EMERALD ASH BORER

On Apr. 21, 2026, Milbank city administrator Steve Pendergrass outlined the financial impact of an invasive emerald ash borer threat, noting that removing affected trees could reach up to $4 million. Milbank's canopy is heavily weighted toward ash - more than half of its trees are ash, including in parks and the cemetery.In response, the city is pursuing a 'herd immunity' strategy designed to fortify the ash canopy over the next 12 years at an estimated cost of about $2.4 million. The approach emphasizes targeted treatment and monitoring to save existing trees while reducing pest spread, a model the city hopes other South Dakota communities can adapt.Local officials, environmental experts, and residents are expected to collaborate as treatments are phased, focusing first on vulnerable areas. Milbank argues the plan balances preservation of green space with proactive pest management, aiming to create a resilient urban forest that can withstand invasive threats.

+ MILBANK ANALYSTS INTERPRET CFTC GUIDANCE ON PREDICTION MARKETS AND IMPLICATIONS FOR REGULATION

Milbank discussed the Commodity Futures Trading Commission’s (CFTC) latest guidance on prediction markets during a webinar held last week, titled 'The CFTC’s New Guidance on Prediction Markets: What You Need to Know,' which took place on Tuesday. The briefing follows the CFTC’s 17th March 2026 announcement introducing a regulatory expectations advisory and an Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking, triggering a 45-day public comment period that ends on 30th April.Milbank Partner Josh Sterling opened by noting the remarkable growth of prediction markets, from $3 billion in trading volume in January 2025 to $27 billion in January 2026, a development that has drawn attention from market participants seeking to rejuvenate markets as well as from courts and state regulators. The session also featured Milbank litigation partners Grant Mainland, Matt Laroche, and John Williams, who discussed the current state of the market and what the CFTC’s announcements could mean for operators.Laroche emphasized that the CFTC's advisory indicates the commission's belief that contracts related to sports events fall under its jurisdiction, a significant clarification. He said, "The purpose of the advisory was to provide right up from that the CFTC is recognizing it is a unique financial asset class that is rapidly increasing and provides a reliable source of information for a variety of uses." This recognition could influence ongoing litigation over how such contracts are classified. Williams noted that the notice of rulemaking represents a strong stance by the CFTC to assert jurisdiction over these contracts, though it does not clearly outline future regulatory directions. He highlighted the extensive list of questions for public comment, inviting input on how event contracts might contribute to hedging, price risk, and information dissemination, adding, "It’s a good sign the commission is thinking about those things." Milbank also outlined practical steps for operators—evaluating contract listings for compliance, establishing relationships with sports leagues, assessing manipulation risks, participating in the rulemaking process, and staying informed about blockchain developments. Mainland provided an overview of more than 20 ongoing court cases related to prediction markets, underscoring the unusual and unsettled legal landscape, particularly on whether the Commodity Exchange Act preempts state law.

+ MILBANK TO HOST BONDHOLDER CALL FOR KENNEDY-WILSON NOTES

Milbank LLP will host a conference call for holders of Kennedy-Wilson, Inc.'s 4.75% senior notes due 2029, 4.75% senior notes due 2030, and 5.00% senior notes due 2031. The call is scheduled for 5th March 2026 at 3:30 p.m. (EST) to address concerns raised by bondholders regarding the Exchange Offer and consent solicitation statement dated 2nd March 2026.Bondholders wishing to participate must provide their detailed contact information and holdings data on a series-by-series basis to ensure inclusion in the discussion.Milbank LLP, a prominent international law firm founded more than 150 years ago in New York, operates with offices in London, Hong Kong, and Sao Paulo, employing cross-practice teams to assist commercial, financial, and industrial enterprises, as well as institutions and governments. In addition to Kennedy-Wilson, the firm has been analyzing developments affecting unsecured creditors of Braskem S.A., underscoring its broader advisory work and commitment to transparent issuer-investor dialogue.
